title: DETR Architecture
concept: DETR pipelines images through a CNN backbone, a Transformer encoder-decoder, and MLP prediction heads.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main outcome of one-to-one matching in DETR?

options.text: It trains the network to output exactly one box per object, removing NMS
It speeds up the convolution speed by 2x
It makes the backbone layers redundant

exercises.explain: Correct! Strict one-to-one matching ensures the model natively predicts a single box per object.

cards.title: The Transformer Pipeline
cards.body: Let's trace a photo step-by-step through the entire DETR architecture. How do CNN backbones, encoders, decoders, and prediction heads connect to form the pipeline?

cards.title: DETR Architecture Blocks
cards.body: DETR consists of: 1) a CNN backbone (features), 2) a Transformer Encoder (pixel relation modeling), 3) a Transformer Decoder (cross-attention with object queries), and 4) a Feed-Forward Network (MLP heads predicting class and coordinates).

cards.title: The Publishing House
cards.body: Think of DETR like a publishing house: the photographer takes a photo (CNN backbone), the editor relates all parts of the photo together (Encoder self-attention), the writer drafts stories using specific questions (Decoder queries), and the printer formats the final layout (Prediction heads).

cards.title: DETR Feature Processing
cards.body: ResNet outputs a 14x14 feature map (196 pixels). The encoder flattens these into 196 tokens and applies self-attention. The decoder takes 100 object queries, relates them to the 196 tokens using cross-attention, and outputs 100 vectors to the classification and box heads.

cards.title: No Self-Attention on Pixels?
cards.body: You might think self-attention is only for NLP. Fact: DETR's encoder uses self-attention to relate every pixel token to every other pixel token. This allows the model to capture global context (e.g. realizing a boat is a boat because it's surrounded by water features).

cards.title: DETR Architecture Flow
cards.body: Step-by-step path from image to predictions:

left.text:
CNN extracts 2D features
Encoder applies self-attention
Captures global image context

right.text:
Decoder matches queries to features
Cross-attention finds objects
MLP predicts coordinates & class

exercises.prompt: What does the Transformer Encoder in DETR do?

options.text: It applies self-attention to model global relationships between all pixel features
It performs max pooling to shrink the image
It matches predicted boxes to ground truth labels
It crops the image into 100 patches

exercises.explain: Correct! The encoder uses self-attention to model relationships across the entire image features globally.

exercises.prompt: True or False: DETR uses Feed-Forward Networks (MLPs) as prediction heads to output class probabilities and bounding box coordinates.
exercises.explain: True. The decoder outputs are passed to separate linear projection layers (MLPs) to generate classes and coordinates.

exercises.sentence: The encoder uses self-attention on pixels, while the decoder uses ___ attention to relate object queries to image features.
exercises.explain: Cross-attention matches the decoder's learnable queries with the encoder's feature representations.

exercises.prompt: How does global self-attention in the encoder help detect partially hidden objects?

options.text: By using context from surrounding pixels (like water around a boat) to assist classification
By filling in the missing pixels with white color
By scaling the image resolution up

exercises.explain: Correct! Global context allows the model to reason about object relationships (e.g. a cup is likely near a plate).

exercises.prompt: You are building a DETR model. You notice that the Transformer Encoder is using 90% of the memory. Why does the encoder have such a massive memory footprint?

options.text: Self-attention calculates relations between all pairs of pixel tokens, resulting in quadratic complexity relative to the feature map size.
The encoder is storing all training images.
The base ResNet backbone is running backward.
The decoder queries are copying themselves.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Standard self-attention has O(N^2) complexity, making high-resolution encoder processing extremely memory-intensive. Phase 8: Modern Object Detection is complete. Next up: Image Segmentation.
